title: Trna/pre-mirna compositions and use in treating cancer
abstract: Provided are hybrid tRNA/pre-miRNA molecules, e.g. , comprising a single tRNA and one, two or more pre-miRNA molecules, useful for the production and therapeutic delivery of an inserted RNA sequence, e.g. , one or more miRNAs. Also provided are liposomes and nanoparticles that include the hybrid tRNA/pre-miRNA molecules. Methods of treating cancer by administration of the hybrid tRNA/pre-miRNA molecules are also provided.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A polynucleotide comprising a tRNA operably linked to two or more pre-microRNA (pre-miRNA), wherein each of the two or more pre-miRNA are operably linked to an inserted RNA molecule that is heterologous to the pre-miRNA. 2. The polynucleotide of claim 1, wherein all or part of the stem-loop anticodon of the tRNA is replaced with the pre-miRNA. 3. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 2, wherein the inserted RNA molecule is inserted at, abutted with or operably linked to: a) the 5' end of the pre-miRNA; b) the 3' end of the pre-miRNA; c) 5' of a dicer or RNase cleavage site of the pre-miRNA; or d) 3' of a dicer or RNase cleavage site of the pre-miRNA. 4. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the polynucleotide is from about 275 nucleotides, e.g., from about 280 nucleotides, e.g., from about 290 nucleotides and up to about 400 nucleotides in length. 5. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the tRNA is a tRNA derived from or coding for an amino acid selected from the group consisting of serine, leucine, glycine, glutamate, aspartate, glutamine, arginine, cysteine, lysine, methionine, asparagine, alanine, histidine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, proline, tryptophan, tyrosine, threonine, and valine. 6. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the RNA coding for the tRNA comprises a 5' tRNA sequence and a 3' tRNA sequence having at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to SEQ ID NOs: 300-355, as provided in Table 8. 7. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the tRNA is a mammalian tRNA, e.g., a human tRNA. 8. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the two or more pre-microRNA are derived from human pre-miRNA molecules. 9. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the two or more pre-miRNA are selected from the group consisting of pre-miR-34a, pre-miR-l24, pre- miR-l29l, pre-miR-200b, pre-miR-200a, pre-miR-l4l, pre-miR-429, pre-miR-l33a, pre- let-7c, pre-miR-l25a, pre-miR-328, pre-miR-l26, pre-miR-298, pre-miR-l48, pre-miR-l44, pre-miR-l, pre-miR-l33, pre-miR-888, pre-miR-6775, pre-miR-374, pre-miR-92, pre-miR- 1180, pre-miR-2l8, pre-miR-7, pre-miR-378, pre-miR-l7, pre-miR-l8a, pre-miR-22, pre- miR-l22, pre-miR-30b, pre-miR-449, pre-miR-506, pre-miR-98, pre-miR-4458, pre-miR- 206, pre-miR-5l9, pre-miR-93, pre-miR-l06, pre-miR-373, and pre-miR-520. 10. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the two or more pre-miRNA are derived from the same pre-miRNA molecules. 11. The polynucleotide of claim 10, wherein the two or more pre-miRNA are derived from human pre-miR-34a molecules. 12. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the two or more pre-miRNA are derived from different pre-miRNA molecules. 13. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the inserted RNA is selected from the group consisting of a noncoding RNA (ncRNA), mature microRNA (miRNA), a small interfering RNA (siRNA), a short hairpin RNA (shRNA), a Piwi-interacting RNA (piRNA), a small nuclear RNA (snRNA), a small nucleolar RNA (snoRNA), a guide RNA (gRNA), an antisense RNA (asRNA), a small activating RNA (saRNA), a catalytic RNA, a riboswitch, an RNA aptamer. 14. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein the inserted RNA has at least about 18 nucleotides and up to about 200 nucleotides. 15. The polynucleotide of claim 14, wherein the inserted RNA has at least about 18 nucleotides and up to about 50 nucleotides. 16. The polynucleotide of claim 14, wherein the inserted RNA has at least about 20 nucleotides and up to about 25 nucleotides. 17. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the inserted RNA is a mature miRNA. 18. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein the inserted RNA comprise two or more mature miRNA selected from the group consisting of let-7c, miR-298, miR-2l6, miR-34a, miR-l24, miR-328, miR-l44, miR-l26, miR-l6, miR-l8, miR-l25a, miR-l95, miR-l99a, miR-200, miR-224, miR-l29l, miR-429, miR-l48, miR- 144, miR-l, miR-l33, miR-888, miR-6775, miR-374, miR-92, miR-1 180, miR-2l8, miR-7, miR-378, miR-l7, miR-l8a, miR-22, miR-l22, miR-30b, miR-449, miR-506, miR-98, miR- 4458, miR-206, miR-5l9, miR-93, miR-l06, miR-373, and miR-520. 19. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the inserted RNA in the two or more pre-miRNA are the same. 20. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the inserted RNA in the two or more pre-miRNA are different. 21. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the inserted RNA are selected from the group consisting of let-7c, miR-l29l, miR-200, miR-92, miR- 34a and miR-124. 22. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the inserted RNA are mature miRNA selected from the group consisting of miR-l29l, miR-34, miR- 124, miR-200, and miR-2l6. 23. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the inserted RNA are mature miRNA selected from the group consisting of let-7c, miR-298, miR-2l6, miR-l24, miR-328, miR-l44, miR-l26, miR-l6, miR-l8, miR-l25a, miR-l95, miR-l99a, miR-200, and miR-224. 24. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 23, further wherein the tRNA and/or pre-miRNA are operably linked to one or more aptamers, small activating RNAs (saRNAs), or catalytic RNAs. 25. The polynucleotide of claim 24, wherein the aptamer, saRNA or catalytic RNA is inserted at, abutted with or operably linked to: a) the 5' end of the pre-miRNA; b) the 3' end of the pre-miRNA; c) 5' of a dicer or RNase cleavage site of the pre-miRNA; or d) 3' of a dicer or RNase cleavage site of the pre-miRNA. 26. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 24 to 25, wherein the aptamer binds to a target antigen selected from the group consisting of sephedex, EpCAM, VEGF, fms related tyrosine kinase 1 (FLT1), theophylline, malachite green, HCC-22-5, keratin 23 (KRT23), alpha 2-HS glycoprotein (AHSG), ferritin light chain (FTL), MAGE- Al, MAGE-A3/4, NY-ESO-l, 14-3-3z, c-Myc, MDM2, NPM1, pl6, p53, cyclin Bl, KIF20A, MUC1, CA 19-9, DU-PAN-2, TAG-72, cadherin 3 (CDH3)/P-cadherin, Receptor binding cancer antigen expressed on SiSo cells (RCAS1), and SC6. 27. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 24 to 26, wherein the aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence comprising at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to one of SEQ ID NOs: 356- 359. 28. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 27, wherein the polynucleotide comprises at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to any one of SEQ ID NOs: 183-210, 265-288 and 296-298. 29. A polynucleotide comprising a tRNA operably linked to a pre- miRNA, wherein the polynucleotide comprises at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to any one of SEQ ID NOs: 85-182 and 211-264. 30. A polynucleotide comprising a human tRNA operably linked to a pre-miRNA comprising an inserted RNA molecule, e.g., that is heterologous to the pre- miRNA, wherein the polynucleotide comprises at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to any one of SEQ ID NOs: 289- 295. 31. The polynucleotide of claim 30, wherein all or part of the stem- loop anticodon of the tRNA is replaced with the pre-miRNA. 32. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 31, wherein the polynucleotide is from about 150 nucleotides, e.g., from about 160 nucleotides, e.g., from about 170 nucleotides, e.g., from about 180 nucleotides, and up to about 230 nucleotides in length. 33. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 32, wherein the tRNA is a tRNA derived from or coding for an amino acid selected from the group consisting of serine, leucine, glycine, glutamate, aspartate, glutamine, arginine, cysteine, lysine, methionine, asparagine, alanine, histidine, isoleucine, phenylalanine, proline, tryptophan, tyrosine, threonine, and valine. 34. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 33, wherein the RNA coding for the tRNA comprises a 5' tRNA sequence and a 3' tRNA sequence having at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to SEQ ID NOs: 300-355, as provided in Table 8. 35. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 34, wherein the tRNA is a mammalian tRNA, e.g., a human tRNA. 36. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 35, wherein the inserted RNA is selected from the group consisting of a noncoding RNA (ncRNA), mature microRNA (miRNA), a small interfering RNA (siRNA), a short hairpin RNA (shRNA), a Piwi-interacting RNA (piRNA), a small nuclear RNA (snRNA), a small nucleolar RNA (snoRNA), a guide RNA (gRNA), an antisense RNA (asRNA), a small activating RNA (saRNA), a catalytic RNA, a riboswitch, and an RNA aptamer. 37. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 36, wherein the inserted RNA has at least about 18 nucleotides and up to about 200 nucleotides. 38. The polynucleotide of claim 37, wherein the inserted RNA has at least about 18 nucleotides and up to about 50 nucleotides. 39. The polynucleotide of claim 37, wherein the inserted RNA has at least about 20 nucleotides and up to about 25 nucleotides. 40. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 39, wherein the inserted RNA is a mature miRNA. 41. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 40, wherein the inserted RNA is a mature miRNA selected from the group consisting of let-7c, miR-298, miR-2l6, miR-34a, miR-l24, miR-328, miR-l44, miR-l26, miR-l6, miR-l8, miR-l25a, miR-l95, miR-l99a, miR-200, miR-224, miR-l29l, miR-429, miR-l48, miR-l44, miR-l, miR-l33, miR-888, miR-6775, miR-374, miR-92, miR-l 180, miR-2l8, miR-7, miR-378, 

 miR-l7, miR-l8a, miR-22, miR-l22, miR-30b, miR-449, miR-506, miR-98, miR-4458, miR-206, miR-5l9, miR-93, miR-l06, miR-373, and miR-520. 42. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 41, further wherein the tRNA and/or pre-miRNA are operably linked to an aptamer, a small activating RNA (saRNA), or a catalytic RNA. 43. The polynucleotide of claim 42, wherein the aptamer, saRNA or catalytic RNA is inserted at, abutted with or operably linked to: a) the 5' end of the pre-miRNA; b) the 3' end of the pre-miRNA; c) 5' of a dicer or RNase cleavage site of the pre-miRNA; or d) 3' of a dicer or RNase cleavage site of the pre-miRNA. 44. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 43, wherein the aptamer binds to a target antigen selected from the group consisting of sephedex, EpCAM, VEGF, fms related tyrosine kinase 1 (FLT1), theophylline, malachite green, HCC-22-5, keratin 23 (KRT23), alpha 2-HS glycoprotein (AHSG), ferritin light chain (FTL), MAGE- Al, MAGE-A3/4, NY-ESO-l, 14-3-3z, c-Myc, MDM2, NPM1, pl6, p53, cyclin Bl, KIF20A, MUC1, CA 19-9, DU-PAN-2, TAG-72, cadherin 3 (CDH3)/P-cadherin, Receptor binding cancer antigen expressed on SiSo cells (RCAS1), and SC6. 45. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 30 to 44, wherein the aptamer comprises a nucleic acid sequence comprising at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to one of SEQ ID NOs: 356- 359. 46. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 45, comprising at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to SEQ ID NOs:85-298. 47. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 46, wherein the pre- miRNA is naturally or artificially derived. 48. The polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 47, wherein the polynucleotide is substantially non-immunogenic to a mammal. 49. An expression cassette comprising the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48. 50. A liposome or a nanoparticle comprising the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48 or the expression cassette of claim 49. 51. A viral vector comprising the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48 or the expression cassette of claim 49. 52. The liposome or nanoparticle of claim 50, wherein the liposome comprises an inner core comprising the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48 complexed with a polyethylenimine (PEI) and an outer lipid bilayer. 53. The liposome or nanoparticle of any one of claims 50 to 52, wherein the inner core comprises a liposomal-branched polyethylenimine (PEI) polyplex (LPP), e.g., having a molecular weight of about 10,000 daltons. 54. The liposome or nanoparticle of any one of claims 50 to 53, wherein the outer lipid bilayer comprises a mixture of l,2-di-0-octadecenyl-3-trimethylammonium propane (DOTMA), cholesterol and l,2-Dimyristoyl-sn-glycerol, methoxypolyethylene glycol (DMG-PEG2000). 55. A host cell transfected or transformed with the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48 or the expression cassette of claim 49 or the liposome or nanoparticle of any one or claims 50 to 54. 56. The host cell of claim 55, wherein the host cell is a prokaryotic cell or a eukaryotic cell. 57. The host cell of any one of claims 55 to 56, wherein the host cell is selected from a bacterial cell, a mammalian cell, an insect cell or a plant cell. 58. A method of preventing, mitigating, reducing, reversing and/or inhibiting the growth, proliferation, and/or progression of cancer in a subject in need thereof, comprising administering to the subject the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48 or the expression cassette of claim 49 or the liposome, nanoparticle or viral vector of any one or claims 50 to 54. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the cancer is selected from the group consisting of breast cancer, lymphoma, colorectal cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma, pancreatic cancer, prostate cancer, and lung cancer. 60. The method of any one of claims 58 to 59, wherein the polynucleotide comprises one or more mature miRNAs selected from the group consisting of let-7c, miR-298, miR-2l6, miR-34a, miR-l24, miR-328, miR-l44, miR-l26, miR-l6, miR-l8, miR-l25a, miR-l95, miR-l99a, miR-200, miR-224, miR-l29l, miR-429, miR- 148, miR-l44, miR-l, miR-l33, miR-888, miR-6775, miR-374, miR-92, miR-H80, miR- 218, miR-7, miR-378, miR-l7, miR-l8a, miR-22, miR-l22, miR-30b, miR-449, miR-506, miR-98, miR-4458, miR-206, miR-5l9, miR-93, miR-l06, miR-373, and miR-520. 61. The method of any one of claims 58 to 60, wherein the cancer is lung cancer and the polynucleotide comprises one or more mature miRNAs selected from the group consisting of miR-34a and miR-124. 62. The method of any one of claims 58 to 60, wherein the cancer is pancreatic cancer and the polynucleotide comprises one or more mature miRNAs selected from the group consisting of miR-l29l, miR-34, miR-l24, miR-200, and miR-2l6. 63. The method of any one of claims 58 to 60, wherein the cancer is hepatocellular carcinoma and the polynucleotide comprises one or more mature miRNAs selected from the group consisting of let-7c, miR-298, miR-2l6, miR-l24, miR-328, miR- 144, miR-l26, miR-l6, miR-l8, miR-l25a, miR-l95, miR-l99a, miR-200, and miR-224. 64. The method of any one of claims 58 to 63, wherein the polynucleotide, liposome or nanoparticle is administered via a route selected from intravenously, intraarterially, intraperitoneally, intraperitoneally, intrapulmonarily, intrahepatically, subcutaneously or intratumorally. 65. The method of any one of claims 58 to 64, wherein a therapeutic regimen of the polynucleotide, liposome or nanoparticle is administered is administered multiple times, e.g., daily, weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, e.g., until a predetermined or desired endpoint is reached. 66. The method of any one of claims 58 to 65, wherein the subject is exhibiting symptoms of cancer, e.g., has one or more tumors. 67. The method of any one of claims 58 to 65, wherein the subject is in remission and is at risk of redeveloping tumors. 68. The method of any one of claims 58 to 67, further comprising co administration of one or more chemotherapeutic or anticancer agents. 69. The method of any one of claims 58 to 68, wherein the subject is tested for the overexpression or underexpression of one or more miRNAs prior to administrationÂ· 70. The method of any one of claims 58 to 69, comprising administering to the subject one or more hybrid tRNA/pre-miRNA molecules having at least about 90%, 91%, 92%, 93%, 94%, 95%, 96%, 97%, 98%, 99% or 100% sequence identity to SEQ ID NOs:85-298. 71. A kit comprising the polynucleotide of any one of claims 1 to 48 or the expression cassette of claim 49, the liposome, nanoparticle or viral vector of any one or claims 50 to 54, and or the host cell of any one of claims 55 to 57.
